senses.definition: A historical or literary term referring to a type of fragrant resin obtained from certain trees, used in perfumery and medicine.
examples: The spice and scent of benjoin filled the cabinet.
In ancient texts, benjoin is listed among precious aromatics.

senses.definition: A given name, often a nickname for Benjamin; used as a proper name for a person or fictional character.
examples: Benjy arrived early to help with the project.
In the novel, Benjy is a sensitive, observant character.

senses.definition: A botanical term (likely a misspelling or obscure name) with no widely recognized senses in contemporary English.
The most benevolent or favorable among a group; the best or most favorable position in a context.
examples: This is benmost option available to us.
In the council, her suggestion was benmost for community interests.

senses.definition: A surname or archaic spelling variant; also appears as a given name in some contexts.
examples: Mr. Benn shows a steady hand in negotiations.
The Benn family gathered for the reunion.

senses.definition: A plant also known as Sesamum indicum, used for its seeds in cooking; commonly called sesame.
examples: I sprinkled benne seeds on the bread for extra crunch.
Benne oil is used in certain regional dishes.

senses.definition: A plant name, possibly a regional or misspelled form of “bennel” referring to bittersweet or a plant in the buckwheat family; not standard in modern English.
Plural form likely referring to multiple entities named Benn or a plant/term with uncertain usage.
A surname or given name; also can refer to a type of plant (e.g., Bennet's herb) in historical contexts.
examples: Mr. Bennet is the family patriarch.
Bennet's herb was once prized in apothecaries.

senses.definition: Plural of Bennet; indicates multiple individuals or items named Bennet.
examples: The Bennets attended the reunion.
Several Bennets were listed in the registry.

senses.definition: A plant family name Bennettitaceae in scientific taxonomy.
examples: Bennettitaceae is a small family of extinct plants.

senses.definition: Relating to the Bennettitaceae plant family or its characteristics.
examples: Fossils show bennettitaceous features.

senses.definition: An extinct order of seed plants; Bennettitales, known from fossils.
examples: Fossil records of bennettales reveal ancient plant life.

senses.definition: A fossil plant from the Bennettitales with characteristic features; sometimes referred to as bennetite or bennetite fossils.
examples: The bennetite fossil resembles ancient cycads.

senses.definition: A common name for a weed or plant species in certain regions; may refer to a species within the genus Centaurea or related groups depending on locale.
examples: The field was crowded with bennetweed in late summer.

senses.definition: A type of traditional Indian currency or a seed (sesame) used as a spice or garnish; also a given name in some cultures.
examples: The spice market sold benni seeds in small bags.
Benni is a common nickname in some communities.

senses.definition: Plural of Benny; informal plural referring to people named Benny or the nickname itself.
examples: The Bennies gathered at the cafe.
Two bennies were spotted at the concert.

senses.definition: A surname or given name; may refer to people with the last name or first name Bennis.
examples: Dr. Bennis published a paper on leadership.

senses.definition: Sesame seed; a culinary ingredient obtained from Sesamum indicum.
examples: I sprinkled benniseed on top of the bread.

senses.definition: A diminutive or nickname for Benjamin; also refers to Benny (slang) for a friend or buddy; can be a form of money (slang) in some contexts.
examples: Hey Benny, want to grab coffee?
The team nicknamed him Benny after the founder.

senses.definition: A chemical solvent or drug (benzoic derivatives) or a brand name; in some contexts,
appears as shorthand.
examples: The lab used beno as a solvent in the old procedure.

senses.definition: A systemic fungicide formerly used in agriculture; may be restricted or banned in many regions due to health concerns.
examples: Benomyl was widely used before its safety concerns were fully understood.

senses.definition: Plural of benomyl; several fungicide products or references in literature.
examples: The lab tested several benomyls for residue levels.

senses.definition: A directional or positional term meaning toward the north side, or more broadly toward the top or upper part of something.
examples: Walk benorth along the river until you reach the bridge.

senses.definition: A musical note in the bass clef named for its location; also a loanword in some languages; may appear in specialized music notation.
examples: The benote marks the end of the phrase.
